I’ll keep this read-only and anchor the review on the `Pool::complete` diff, then trace the surrounding `Lease` behavior and any tests/callers that make the failure concrete.Prism navigation tools are not exposed in this session, so I’m using direct file reads and search only. Next I’m checking the pool implementation, tests, and references to `complete`/`release`.The core trace is already visible: `complete` calls `release` and then drops a `Lease` whose `Drop` also calls `release`. I’m doing one more read of the diff/package metadata to make sure there are no existing tests or alternate call paths that change the conclusion.There are no separate test files in this fixture; the crate is just `src/lib.rs` plus `Cargo.toml`. I have enough to finish the review without running anything or changing files.- BLOCKER [WRONG] `src/lib.rs:36-38`, `Pool::complete`: this returns the same slot twice. For a size-1 pool, `checkout()` takes `available` to 0; `complete(lease)` calls `release()` at line 37, then `drop(lease)` triggers `Lease::drop` at line 49 and releases again, leaving `available == 2`. That lets two later `checkout()` calls succeed from a one-slot pool. Fix by removing the explicit `self.release()` and relying on consuming/dropping the lease, or remove `complete` entirely.

- MAJOR [WRONG] `src/lib.rs:36-38`, `Pool::complete` receiver/signature: the method can complete a lease from a different pool and corrupt the receiver. With `pool_a` and `pool_b`, `pool_b.complete(lease_from_pool_a)` increments `pool_b.available` even though no `pool_b` slot was checked out, then drops the lease and releases `pool_a`. Fix by not taking `self` for completion, or validate the lease belongs to `self` before doing anything; the simplest safe version is just consuming the lease and letting `Drop` run.

Verdict: BLOCKED; the new `complete` violates the exact-once slot return contract.
